streams.reasoning: From Chemistry Plan stream_logic: dipolarophile in DES, separated from nitrile oxide precursor to prevent premature reaction. Concentration matches batch protocol.
From Chemistry Plan stream_logic: nitrile oxide precursor in DES, kept separate until T-mixer. Higher concentration (1.0 M vs 0.5 M) to deliver 2.0 equiv in smaller volume.

mixing_order_reasoning: From Chemistry Plan: Streams A and B combined at T-mixer immediately before heated coil reactor. This ensures transient nitrile oxide intermediate generated from ethyl nitroacetate at 120°C is immediately trapped by phenylacetylene, minimizing nitrile oxide dimerization to furoxan.
pre_reactor_steps: Preheat feed lines to 60°C to improve DES pumpability (viscosity ~50-100 cP at RT)
post_reactor_steps: Cool outlet stream to room temperature to stop reaction
chemistry_notes: Thermal 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition with in-situ nitrile oxide generation. Key design: separate streams prevent premature nitrile oxide formation/dimerization. DES viscosity requires preheated feed lines. No photochemistry involved - purely thermal mechanism. BPR required due to high temperature (120°C) and potential water generation from nitrile oxide formation. Calculations show kinetically controlled regime (Da = 0.492) with excellent plug flow (Pe = 691).

reasoning_per_field.residence_time_min: Calculation estimated 60.0 min (range 1.0-90.0 min) via analogy. Analogy 2 used 20 min for [3+2] cycloaddition but at lower temperature (60°C vs 120°C). Analogy 3 used 179 min at 154°C but different mechanism. Using calculation result of 60 min as it accounts for our specific kinetics.
reasoning_per_field.flow_rate_mL_min: Calculation: total flow = reactor_volume/residence_time = 30.0 mL / 60.0 min = 0.5 mL/min. Stream A: 0.167 mL/min, Stream B: 0.333 mL/min to deliver proper stoichiometry.
reasoning_per_field.temperature_C: Same as batch (120°C) - Chemistry Plan indicates this temperature required for nitrile oxide generation. No adjustment needed.
reasoning_per_field.concentration_M: Calculation result: 0.067 M in reactor after mixing. Lower than batch (0.5 M) due to flow dilution but sufficient for reaction.
reasoning_per_field.BPR_bar: Calculation required 5.0 bar. High temperature (120°C) and potential water generation from nitrile oxide formation necessitates BPR to maintain liquid phase.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_material: PFA chosen for high temperature compatibility (120°C) and chemical resistance to DES. Not photochemistry so transparency not required.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_ID_mm: Calculation used 1.0 mm giving Re = 10.6 (laminar flow), Pe = 691 (excellent plug flow), reasonable pressure drop (0.13 bar).
reasoning_per_field.reactor_volume_mL: Calculation result: 30.0 mL. Matches Analogy 1 reactor volume, appropriate for 60 min residence time.
reasoning_per_field.light_setup: Empty string - Chemistry Plan confirms no photochemistry involved, purely thermal mechanism.
reasoning_per_field.wavelength_nm: Null - no light required for thermal cycloaddition reaction.
